pattern: A recursive evaluator tokenizes parenthesised expressions and threads a chained scope so let bindings shadow outer ones and are restored when the scope ends.
hints: An expression is either a literal integer, a bare variable, or a parenthesised operator form (let, add, mult).
Splitting a parenthesised body into top-level tokens means tracking parenthesis depth so nested expressions stay intact.
Carry a scope mapping; for let, evaluate each value-then-bind pair in sequence and finally the last token, undoing the new bindings before returning so outer scopes are unaffected.

input: (let x 2 (mult x (let x 3 y 4 (add x y))))
(let x 1 y 2 x (add x y) (add x y))

stress.description: build random valid nested expressions of add, mult and let with bound variables
stress.note: scope list ensures referenced variables are always bound
